messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "cra-conformity-clock-outlasts-yam-window",
 "title": "Cyber Resilience Act (Reg. EU 2024/2847): the conformity clock for a networked PDU, started today, ends after the Year-1 window closes",
 "narrative": "An intelligent PDU is a 'product with digital elements' under the CRA. Vulnerability-handling and 24h ENISA incident-reporting obligations apply from 11 September 2026 and full conformity (CE marking against CRA essential requirements, SBOM, coordinated vulnerability disclosure, support-period declaration) from 11 December 2027 — squarely overlapping a pre-launch venture's first 12 months. The harmonized standards CEN/CENELEC must deliver are not yet published, and remote-management functionality risks classifying the product as an 'important product' requiring third-party assessment through a notified-body queue that does not yet exist at scale. Worse, data-center buyers front-run: because PDUs installed in 2026 live in racks past 2027, procurement specs are already demanding CRA-readiness attestations today. The serviceableFactor's 0.55 haircut was built on the old CE/TÜV/ecodesign stack; it prices none of this, and the obtainableFactor's 1% Year-1 pace assumes the product can be quoted into 2026 tenders at all.",
 "category": "exogenous",
 "targetNodes": [
  "serviceableFactor",
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "CRA obligations land inside the entry window: reporting duties from Sept 2026, full conformity Dec 2027, with harmonized standards unpublished and notified-body capacity unbuilt — a fixed regulatory calendar colliding with a pre-launch stage",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Product",
    "profile:Stage",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Large operators and colos, whose gear outlives the 2027 deadline, insert CRA-readiness clauses into 2026 RFQs; the existing cert-stack rationale for serviceableFactor ('justifies <1.0 but doesn't quantify 0.55') already shows the model treats compliance as a static haircut, not a moving gate",
   "refs": [
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c4",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Year-1 tenders that are contestable at all are already scarce and pre-locked 12–24+ months ahead; adding a certification artifact the entrant cannot yet produce removes it from the shortlists that make up the 1% anchor",
   "refs": [
    "digest:refine-demand-discontinuity.prelet-pipeline-lockout#c0",
    "digest:refine-competitive-foreclosure.bundling-eliminates-bakeoffs-year1#c0",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "Reach shrinks (CRA-gated demand exits the serviceable pool) and pace slows (Year-1 obtainable share falls below the 1% anchor while incumbents ship CRA-ready roadmaps)",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "'CE marking' is already a checked box on the founder's compliance list, and the CRA presents itself as just another CE regulation. The trap is procedural, not substantive: the conformity ROUTE changes (SBOM, vulnerability process, possible third-party assessment) and buyers enforce it via procurement one to two years before the legal deadline — so the risk bites in 2026 sales cycles while the founder's calendar says 'deadline 2027'.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Three 2026 CE data-center PDU RFQs (operator or colo) whose specification documents contain no CRA/cyber-conformity or SBOM requirement, plus confirmation that networked PDUs fall in the CRA default (self-assessment) class.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.55,
  "rationale": "The CRA dates and product scope are enacted law, not forecast; residual uncertainty is only the important-product classification of remote-management PDUs and how aggressively CE buyers front-run the 2027 deadline in 2026 specs.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.4,
   "note": "CRA-gated (attestation-demanding) demand exits the entrant's reachable pool until conformity artifacts exist"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.006,
   "note": "Year-1 pace falls below the 1% anchor as 2026 shortlists require CRA-readiness"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "CRA harmonized-standards progress for network/management devices and the important-product classification of remote-access equipment",
   "where": "CEN/CENELEC JTC 13 work programme and the EU Official Journal harmonized-standards citations; Commission CRA implementing-act docket",
   "threshold": "Networked PDUs / remote management listed under Annex III important products, or harmonized standards slipping past mid-2026",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "CRA/SBOM clauses appearing in data-center electrical RFQs and incumbent price lists",
   "where": "TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) EU data-center fit-out tenders; Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton product-security pages and price-list footnotes announcing CRA-ready SKUs",
   "threshold": "≥2 CE tenders requiring vendor SBOM/CRA attestation, or an incumbent marketing 'CRA-ready' as a differentiator",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a CRA classification and gap assessment (product class, assessment route, notified-body need) with a certified test house now, before design freeze",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": "Determines whether third-party assessment — the long pole — is required at all"
  },
  {
   "action": "Build the SBOM, secure-development and vulnerability-disclosure artifacts into the launch release and sell them as a differentiator against incumbents' legacy firmware",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Turns the 2026 buyer front-running from a gate into a wedge"
  },
  {
   "action": "Sequence Year-1 pipeline toward buyers not yet writing CRA clauses (mid-market enterprise retrofit) while conformity completes",
   "type": "operational",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "regulatory-gauntlet",
 "riskId": "risk.regulatory-gauntlet.cra-conformity-clock-outlasts-yam-window"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
